$comment: Single source of truth for the chorograph contract: node/edge kinds, the tag grammar, the containment matrix, and the graph.json wire format. Every language package generates its types and constants from this file (`pnpm codegen`); CI fails if generated code drifts. Edit this, never the *.gen.* files.

nodeKinds.doc: a bounded context / grouping — the only kind that exists purely to contain others
a deployable process: API server, worker, consumer
an API surface a service exposes: HTTP route, RPC method, GraphQL field
a function inside a service that is architecturally significant
scheduled or background work owned by a service
a table / collection inside a database
a queue or topic: SQS, Kafka, Rabbit
a named domain event that flows between services
a third-party system you don't operate: Stripe, SendGrid

edgeKinds.doc: synchronous invocation: HTTP, RPC, an in-process call
depends on, when no sharper verb fits: an external API, a cache

Node.doc: A single thing on the map.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: Stable slug path derived from names: `commerce/orders/post-orders`.

fields.name: parent
fields.doc: Containment parent id, or `null` for a top-level node.

fields.name: description
fields.doc: One or two sentences on what this thing is for. Shown in the detail panel.

fields.name: file
fields.doc: Where the declaring comment lives, relative to where chorograph ran.

Edge.doc: A directed connection between two nodes. `from` is the thing doing the verb, `to` is the thing the verb is done to.

fields.name: label
fields.doc: Optional annotation: protocol (`HTTP`, `gRPC`) or a short verb phrase — the why.

GraphCounts.doc: How many of each kind the graph holds; a cheap summary for tooling.

GraphMeta.doc: Provenance and summary for a generated graph.

fields.name: version
fields.doc: Version of the generator that produced the file.

fields.name: name
fields.doc: The system name from the `@system` comment.

Graph.doc: The complete, serialisable map. This is the on-disk `graph.json` contract.
